CGAL::Mean_curvature_flow_skeletonizatio.name: CGAL::Mean_curvature_flow_skeletonization
CGAL::Mean_curvature_flow_skeletonizatio.brief: Function object that enables to extract the mean curvature flow skeleton of a triangulated surface mesh.
CGAL::Mean_curvature_flow_skeletonizatio.detailed: The algorithm used takes as input a triangulated surface mesh and iteratively contracts the surface mesh following the mean curvature flow [1] . The intermediate contracted surface mesh is called the meso-skeleton . After each iteration, the meso-skeleton is locally remeshed using angle split and edge contraction. The process ends when the modification of the meso-skeleton between two iterations is small. TriangleMesh a model of FaceListGraph Traits a model of MeanCurvatureSkeletonizationTraits Default: CGAL::Kernel_traits< boost::property_traits< boost::property_map<TriangleMesh, CGAL::vertex_point_t>::type >::value_type >::Kernel VertexPointMap a model of ReadWritePropertyMap with boost::graph_traits<TriangleMesh>::vertex_descriptor as key and Traits::Point_3 as value type. Default: boost::property_map<TriangleMesh, CGAL::vertex_point_t>::const_type. SolverTraits_ a model of NormalEquationSparseLinearAlgebraTraits_d . Default: If Eigen 3.2 (or greater) is available and CGAL_EIGEN3_ENABLED is defined, then an overload of Eigen_solver_traits is provided as default parameter: CGAL::Eigen_solver_traits< Eigen::SimplicialLDLT< Eigen::SparseMatrix<double> > >

members.name: Skeleton
members.brief: The graph type representing the skeleton. The vertex property Vmap is a struct with a member point of type Traits::Point_3 and a member vertices of type std::vector<boost::graph_traits<TriangleMesh>::vertex_descriptor> . See the boost documentation page for more details.

members.name: Meso_skeleton
members.brief: When using the low level API it is possible to access the intermediate results of the skeletonization process, called meso-skeleton. It is a triangulated surface mesh which is model of FaceListGraph and HalfedgeListGraph .

members.name: meso_skeleton
members.brief: Reference to the collapsed triangulated surface mesh.

members.name: Mean_curvature_flow_skeletonization
members.brief: The constructor of a skeletonization object.
members.detailed: The algorithm parameters are initialized such that: max_triangle_angle() == 110 quality_speed_tradeoff() == 0.1 medially_centered_speed_tradeoff() == 0.2 area_variation_factor() == 0.0001 max_iterations() == 500 is_medially_centered() == true min_edge_length() == 0.002 * the length of the diagonal of the bounding box of tmesh tmesh is a triangulated surface mesh without borders and has exactly one connected component. tmesh input triangulated surface mesh. vertex_point_map property map which associates a point to each vertex of the graph. traits an instance of the traits class.
members.argsstring: (const TriangleMesh &tmesh, VertexPointMap vertex_point_map=get(CGAL::vertex_point, tmesh), Traits traits=Traits())

members.name: max_triangle_angle
members.brief: During the local remeshing step, a triangle will be split if it has an angle larger than max_triangle_angle() .

members.name: min_edge_length
members.brief: During the local remeshing step, an edge will be collapse if it is length is less than min_edge_length() .

members.name: set_max_triangle_angle
members.brief: set function for max_triangle_angle()

members.name: set_min_edge_length
members.brief: set function for min_edge_length()

members.name: max_iterations
members.brief: Maximum number of iterations performed by contract_until_convergence() .

members.name: area_variation_factor
members.brief: The convergence is considered to be reached if the variation of the area of the meso-skeleton after one iteration is smaller than area_variation_factor() *original_area where original_area is the area of the input triangle mesh.

members.name: set_max_iterations
members.brief: set function for max_iterations()

members.name: set_area_variation_factor
members.brief: set function for area_variation_factor()

members.name: quality_speed_tradeoff
members.brief: This is an advanced function.
members.detailed: Advanced Controls the velocity of movement and approximation quality: decreasing this value makes the mean curvature flow based contraction converge faster, but results in a skeleton of lower quality. This parameter corresponds to $ w_H $ in the original publication.

members.name: is_medially_centered
members.brief: If true , the meso-skeleton placement will be attracted by an approximation of the medial axis of the mesh during the contraction steps, so will be the result skeleton.

members.name: medially_centered_speed_tradeoff
members.brief: This is an advanced function.
members.detailed: Advanced Controls the smoothness of the medial approximation: increasing this value results in a (less smooth) skeleton closer to the medial axis, as well as a lower convergence speed. It is only used if is_medially_centered() ==true . This parameter corresponds to $ w_M $ in the original publication.

members.name: set_quality_speed_tradeoff
members.brief: set function for quality_speed_tradeoff()

members.name: set_is_medially_centered
members.brief: set function for is_medially_centered()

members.name: set_medially_centered_speed_tradeoff
members.brief: set function for medially_centered_speed_tradeoff()

members.name: set_fixed_vertices
members.brief: This is an advanced function.
members.detailed: Advanced sets the vertices in the range [begin, end) as fixed. Fixed vertices will not be moved during contraction and this will therefore prevent convergence towards the skeleton if contract_until_convergence() is used. It is useful only if the end goal is to retrieve the meso-skeleton after a number of contract_geometry() , keeping the specified vertices fixed in place. InputIterator a model of InputIterator with boost::graph_traits<TriangleMesh>::vertex_descriptor as value type.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Creates the curve skeleton: the input surface mesh is iteratively contracted until convergence, and then turned into a curve skeleton.
members.detailed: This is equivalent to calling contract_until_convergence() and convert_to_skeleton() . skeleton graph that will contain the skeleton of the input triangulated surface mesh. For each vertex descriptor vd of skeleton , the corresponding point and the set of input vertices that contracted to vd can be retrieved using skeleton[vd].point and skeleton[vd].vertices respectively.

members.name: contract_geometry
members.brief: Runs one contraction step following the mean curvature flow.

members.name: collapse_edges
members.brief: Collapses edges of the meso-skeleton with length less than min_edge_length() and returns the number of edges collapsed.

members.name: split_faces
members.brief: Splits faces of the meso-skeleton having one angle greater than max_triangle_angle() and returns the number of faces split.

members.name: detect_degeneracies
members.brief: Prevents degenerate vertices to move during the following contraction steps and returns the number of newly fixed vertices.

members.name: contract
members.brief: Performs subsequent calls to contract_geometry() , collapse_edges() , split_faces() and detect_degeneracies() .

members.name: contract_until_convergence
members.brief: Iteratively calls contract() until the change of surface area of the meso-skeleton after one iteration is smaller than area_variation_factor() *original_area where original_area is the area of the input triangle mesh, or if the maximum number of iterations has been reached.

members.name: convert_to_skeleton
members.brief: Converts the contracted surface mesh to a skeleton curve.
members.detailed: Skeleton an instantiation of boost::adjacency_list as a data structure for the skeleton curve. skeleton graph that will contain the skeleton of tmesh . It should be empty before passed to the function.

members.name: m_tmesh
members.brief: The meso-skeleton.

members.name: m_vertex_id_pmap
members.brief: Storing indices of all vertices.

members.name: m_hedge_id_pmap
members.brief: Storing indices of all edges.

members.name: m_tmesh_point_pmap
members.brief: Storing the point for mTriangleMesh vertex_descriptor.

members.name: m_traits
members.brief: Traits class.

members.name: m_omega_H
members.brief: Controlling the velocity of movement and approximation quality.

members.name: m_omega_P
members.brief: Controlling the smoothness of the medial approximation.

members.name: m_min_edge_length
members.brief: Edges with length less than min_edge_length will be collapsed.

members.name: m_alpha_TH
members.brief: Triangles with angle greater than alpha_TH will be split.

members.name: m_zero_TH
members.brief: Value very close to zero.

members.name: m_delta_area
members.brief: contract_until_convergence will stop if the change of area in one iteration is less than delta_area .

members.name: m_original_area
members.brief: Surface area of original surface mesh.

members.name: m_max_iterations
members.brief: Maximum number of iterations.

members.name: m_is_medially_centered
members.brief: Should the skeleton be medially centered?

members.name: m_are_poles_computed
members.brief: Are poles computed?

members.name: m_weight_calculator
members.brief: Cotangent weight calculator.

members.name: m_edge_weight
members.brief: Storing the weights for edges.

members.name: m_solver
members.brief: The sparse solver.

members.name: m_vertex_id_count
members.brief: Assign a unique id to a new vertex.

members.name: m_max_id
members.brief: The maximum id for original surface.
members.detailed: vertices with ids greater than m_max_id are created during split, thus will not be considered in correspondence tracking.

members.name: m_new_id
members.brief: Used when assembling the matrix.

members.name: m_halfedge_angle
members.brief: The incident angle for a halfedge.

members.name: m_normals
members.brief: The normal of surface points.

members.name: init_args
members.brief: Initialize the parameters for Mean_curvature_flow_skeletonization .

members.name: init
members.brief: Initialize some global data structures such as vertex id.

members.name: compute_edge_weight
members.brief: Compute cotangent weights of all edges.

members.name: assemble_LHS
members.brief: Assemble the left hand side.

members.name: assemble_RHS
members.brief: Assemble the right hand side.

members.name: update_vertex_id
members.brief: The order of vertex id is the same as the traverse order.

members.name: update_pole
members.brief: Track correspondent original surface points during collapse.

members.name: compute_incident_angle
members.brief: Compute the incident angles for all the halfedges.

members.name: project_vertex
members.brief: Project the vertex vk to the line of vs and vt .
members.argsstring: (const vertex_descriptor vs, const vertex_descriptor vt, const vertex_descriptor vk, const vertex_descriptor vnew)

members.name: split_flat_triangles
members.brief: Split triangles with an angle greater than alpha_TH .

members.name: detect_degeneracies_in_disk
members.brief: Test degeneracy of a vertex by counting the euler characteristic of its local neighborhood disk.

members.name: compute_voronoi_pole
members.brief: Compute the Voronoi pole for surface vertices. The pole is the furthest vertex in the Voronoi cell containing the given vertex.

members.name: compute_vertex_normal
members.brief: Compute an approximate vertex normal for all vertices.

MeanCurvatureSkeletonizationTraits.name: MeanCurvatureSkeletonizationTraits
MeanCurvatureSkeletonizationTraits.brief: Traits class concept defining the requirements of the class CGAL::Mean_curvature_flow_skeletonization .
MeanCurvatureSkeletonizationTraits.detailed: Has models Any CGAL Kernel with double as Kernel::FT

members.name: Point_3
members.brief: The point type.

members.name: Vector_3
members.brief: The vector type.

members.name: FT
members.brief: The number type. It must be constructible from and convertible to double .

members.name: Construct_point_3
members.brief: Function object type that provides Point_3 operator()(FT x, FT y, FT z) const returning the point with x , y and z as Cartesian coordinates.

members.name: Construct_vector_3
members.brief: Function object type that provides Point_3 operator()(Point_3 p1, Point_3 p2) const returning the vector p1p2 , and Point_3 operator()(NULL_VECTOR) const returning the null vector.

members.name: Construct_scaled_vector_3
members.brief: Function object type that provides Vector_3 operator()(Vector_3 v, FT t) const returning the vector t * v .

members.name: Construct_divided_vector_3
members.brief: Function object type that provides Vector_3 operator()(Vector_3 v, FT t) const returning the vector v / t .

members.name: Construct_cross_product_vector_3
members.brief: Function object type that provides Vector_3 operator()(Vector_3 v1, Vector_3 v2) const returning the cross-product vector of v1 and v2 .

members.name: Construct_sum_of_vectors_3
members.brief: Function object type that provides Vector_3 operator()(Vector_3 v1, Vector_3 v2) const returning the v1+v2 .

members.name: Construct_midpoint_3
members.brief: Function object type that provides Point_3 operator()(Point_3 p1, Point_3 p2) const returning the midpoint of p1 and p2 .

members.name: Compute_squared_distance_3
members.brief: Function object type that provides FT operator()(Point_3 p, Point_3 q) const returning the squared distance between p and q .

members.name: Compute_squared_length_3
members.brief: Function object type that provides FT operator()(Vector_3 v) const returning the squared length of v .

members.name: Compute_area_3
members.brief: Function object type that provides FT operator()(Point_3 p1,Point_3 p2,Point_3 p3) const returning the area of the triangle defined by p1 , p2 and p3 .

members.name: Compute_scalar_product_3
members.brief: Function object type that provides FT operator()(Vector_3 v1, Vector_3 v2) const returning the scalar product of v1 and v2 .

members.name: Compute_x_3
members.brief: Function object type that provides FT operator()(Point_3 p) and FT operator()(Vector_3 v) const returning the x coordinate of a point and a vector respectively.

members.name: Compute_y_3
members.brief: Function object type that provides FT operator()(Point_3 p) and FT operator()(Vector_3 v) const returning the y coordinate of a point and a vector respectively.

members.name: Compute_z_3
members.brief: Function object type that provides FT operator()(Point_3 p) and FT operator()(Vector_3 v) const returning the z coordinate of a point and a vector respectively.
